steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Incident Rehearsal: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Go team handles case 7068 during a mixed-origin fairness and race rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to defend release matrices, end-to-end counts, incident drills, rollback containment, non-claims, and risks; release evidence binds source, modules, build settings, binary or image, configuration, policy, and restored state; then inject one session, secret, SSRF, output, path, log, privacy, or retention fault and contain every derivative. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Rehearse owner revoke, runaway frontier, private redirect, credential leak, parser drift, inaccessible report, goroutine leak, corrupt checkpoint, and unsafe output.

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Defend the Incident Rehearsal model · Theory Incident Rehearsal.
steps.why: Retrieving Incident Rehearsal through the concrete Go crawler production defense dossier case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Authorized site auditing and Go Defend the Incident Rehearsal model · Theory Incident Rehearsal, keep Incident Rehearsal connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Rehearse owner revoke, runaway frontier, private redirect, credential leak, parser drift, inaccessible report, goroutine leak, corrupt checkpoint, and unsafe output.

hints: For Authorized site auditing and Go Defend the Incident Rehearsal model · Theory Incident Rehearsal, name the owner, permission, revision, Go, seed, URL, scope, robots, response, body, tree, record, frontier, goroutine, accessibility, report, cleanup, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Incident Rehearsal precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized site auditing and Go Defend the Incident Rehearsal model · Theory Incident Rehearsal, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory Go team handles case 7068 during a mixed-origin fairness and race rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to defend release matrices, end-to-end counts, incident drills, rollback containment, non-claims, and risks; release evidence binds source, modules, build settings, binary or image, configuration, policy, and restored state; then inject one session, secret, SSRF, output, path, log, privacy, or retention fault and contain every derivative. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Rehearse owner revoke, runaway frontier, private redirect, credential leak, parser drift, inaccessible report, goroutine leak, corrupt checkpoint, and unsafe output.

For Authorized site auditing and Go Defend the Incident Rehearsal model · Theory Incident Rehearsal, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Process restart and rollback resolve every crawler incident.

checks.pattern: // Evidence: crawler-go-incident-rehearsal-1ts13j(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?func\s+evidence_crawler_go_incident_rehearsal_1ts13j\s*\([^)]*\)\s*\(bool,\s*int,\s*string\)\s*\{(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?releaseGates < 24)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?passedGates < 23 \|\| passedGates > releaseGates)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?incidentDrills < 10)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?openCriticalRisks > 0)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
